content: Before acting on a feature/PR branch, verify its base is current and its ticket/issue IDs don't collide with the default branch (git merge-base distance; compare the ticket files). A stale branch can carry ticket IDs that main has since reassigned to different, completed work — editing them is wasted and would clobber on merge. Rebase + renumber to the next free IDs before continuing.
context: This session: PR #4 ('Spec the interactive viewer') was 21 commits behind main; its T-025/T-026 collided with main's completed Path-A T-026 and a different T-025. I edited T-026.json acceptance criteria for several turns before the user flagged 'why are you writing T-026 which is a completed ticket'. Root cause: never compared the branch to current main. Fix applied: rebased onto main and renumbered the branch's tickets to T-028/T-029; later prompt-engineering work used T-030 to stay clear of the unmerged branch.
title: Check a branch's ticket IDs against the default branch before working it — stale branches collide
